title: primula rosea subsp. rhodantha
intro: Primula rosea subsp. rhodantha, also known as the 'rose-leaved primrose', is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The plant's medicinal properties also provide health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Primula rosea subsp. rhodantha is often used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is also added to ointments and creams for topical application.
